vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1455: Codex auth status false-positive when refresh token is burned — widen burn-line matcher to detect refresh_token_reused

plan.title: Codex auth status false-positive when refresh token is burned (dashboard says valid, CLIProxy returns 401)
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: /api/settings/codex-auth returns status: 'valid' while every actual CLIProxy call 401s with 'refresh_token_reused'. The burned-token override in src/lib/codex-auth.ts only matches the literal string 'refresh token has already been used' (line 151), but OpenAI's auth backend also emits the shape 'token refresh failed with status 401: { "code": "refresh_token_reused" }'. PAN-913 closed the first phrasing; this issue extends the same fix to the second. Result today: silent 401s on every GPT-5.x agent spawn, no banner, no notification.
narratives.Proposal: Keep the existing log-tail architecture (the code's own comment at lines 110-112 explicitly rules out HTTP probing — /v1/models always 401s and would generate spurious log lines). Widen the matcher at codex-auth.ts:151 to match BOTH 'refresh token has already been used' AND 'refresh_token_reused'. Add lib-level tests covering both phrasings plus the existing burn-then-success suppression. Add a route-level integration test that points the codex-auth code at a temp log fixture containing 'refresh_token_reused' and asserts the endpoint returns status: 'burned'. No frontend change — CodexAuthBanner already renders for 'burned'.

autoDecisions.summary: Chose log-tail widening (option b in the issue) over HTTP probe (option a) and push-from-cliproxy (option c).
autoDecisions.rationale: The existing comment at src/lib/codex-auth.ts:110-112 documents that HTTP probing is rejected because GET /v1/models always 401s and would generate spurious log lines on every dashboard load. Option (c) requires modifying upstream CLIProxy (out-of-tree). Option (b) is the path the code is already on; this issue is simply that the matcher's literal is too narrow. Smallest blast radius, no new I/O paths, no CLIProxy changes.

autoDecisions.summary: Match both 'refresh token has already been used' AND 'refresh_token_reused' as separate string disjuncts; do not collapse to a regex.
autoDecisions.rationale: Concrete literals are faster to scan than regex over 500 lines and make the two known shapes explicit in code. Future shapes can be added the same way.

autoDecisions.summary: Adapt acceptance criterion AC3 from 'mock CLIProxy returning 401' to 'mock the log tail containing refresh_token_reused'.
autoDecisions.rationale: The existing implementation deliberately does not probe CLIProxy — it tails the log. Testing the actual code path that exists is preferable to introducing a probe path that the code's own comments rule out. Recorded as decision D3 in continue.json.
Matches the project's existing __tests__/ co-located vitest convention (e.g., src/lib/__tests__/agent-directory-cleanup.test.ts, src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/issues-reopen.test.ts).
src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CodexAuthBanner.tsx:13 already renders for status === 'burned'. Fixing the backend matcher is sufficient to satisfy AC2 (banner renders) automatically.

items.title: Widen applyBurnedTokenOverride to detect 'refresh_token_reused' in addition to 'refresh token has already been used'

narrative.Action: In src/lib/codex-auth.ts, modify the burn-line detection at line ~151. Replace the single `line.includes('refresh token has already been used')` check with a disjunction that ALSO matches `line.includes('refresh_token_reused')`. Keep the original literal — never remove it (H1: regression of PAN-913). Add a brief comment immediately above the check naming both known phrasings and citing the two issues (PAN-913 introduced the first, PAN-1455 extends to the second). Do not touch the suppression logic at lines 163-173 (burn-then-success), the staleness window (BURN_STALENESS_MS), or the ignoreBurnBefore handling. Do not introduce a regex — keep two literal `includes()` checks for clarity and speed.

subItems.title: Both literals are present in the source: 'refresh token has already been used' AND 'refresh_token_reused'.
A comment above the matcher names both phrasings and cites PAN-913 and PAN-1455.
The burn-then-200-success suppression at lines 163-173 is unchanged.
Typecheck (`npm run typecheck`) and lint (`npm run lint`) pass.

items.title: Add lib-level tests for checkCodexAuthStatus covering both burn phrasings and the suppression cases

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/__tests__/codex-auth.test.ts (vitest). Use vi.mock or env-var overrides for getCliproxyAuthDir / getCliproxyLogPath so the test writes a fake codex-primary.json (with a future-expiring JWT) and a fake cliproxy.log into a tmp dir. Use vi.setSystemTime so hard-coded log timestamps stay inside the 60-minute BURN_STALENESS_MS window (H5). Generate a JWT whose `exp` claim is ~1 hour in the future and whose `email` field is set. Test cases: (1) credentials missing → status: 'missing'; (2) valid JWT, empty log → status: 'valid'; (3) valid JWT, log contains 'refresh token has already been used' inside window, no later success → status: 'burned' (PAN-913 regression guard); (4) valid JWT, log contains 'refresh_token_reused' inside window, no later success → status: 'burned' (the new case for PAN-1455); (5) valid JWT, log contains 'refresh_token_reused' followed by a 200 on /v1/messages → status: 'valid' (auto-retry won, H3); (6) valid JWT, log contains 'refresh_token_reused' with timestamp older than 60 minutes → status: 'valid' (staleness suppression, H5).

subItems.title: Test case asserts status: 'burned' when log tail contains 'refresh_token_reused' inside the staleness window with no later 200 on /v1/messages.
Test case asserts status: 'burned' when log tail contains 'refresh token has already been used' (PAN-913 regression guard).
Test case asserts status: 'valid' when a 'refresh_token_reused' burn line is followed by a 200 on /v1/messages (suppression preserved).
Test case asserts status: 'valid' when the burn line is older than BURN_STALENESS_MS (60 min).
All tests pass under `npm test` (root vitest run).

items.title: Add route-level integration test asserting GET /api/settings/codex-auth returns 'burned' when log tail contains 'refresh_token_reused'

narrative.Action: Create src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/codex-auth.test.ts (vitest). Follow the pattern of sibling route tests (e.g., issues-reopen.test.ts, request-review-nudge.test.ts). Point the cliproxy auth/log resolvers at a temp directory via env-var overrides or vi.mock. Write a fixture codex-primary.json with a future-expiring JWT and a fixture cliproxy.log whose tail contains a freshly-timestamped `refresh_token_reused` line. Invoke the GET handler for /api/settings/codex-auth (either by mounting the Effect HTTP route in a test harness or by directly calling the underlying checkCodexAuthStatus exported from src/lib/codex-auth.ts via the route's Effect — match the pattern used by neighbouring route tests). Assert the JSON response is { status: 'burned', email, expiresAt }. Cleanup the temp dir in afterEach.

subItems.title: Test exercises the route (or its handler Effect) end-to-end with a temp cliproxy log fixture; does NOT mutate the host's real ~/.panopticon/cliproxy directory.
With log tail containing a fresh 'refresh_token_reused' line and a JWT whose exp is in the future, the response JSON has status === 'burned'.
Companion case: with no burn lines in the log, the same setup returns status === 'valid' (negative control).

verification.notes: Cleared by `pan specialists done review --status passed` override (PAN-1215)

history.notes: Cleared by `pan specialists done review --status passed` override (PAN-1215)
